title: Fixation and Dehydration Protocol for Scanning Electron Microscopy
author: Mario A. Sandoval-Molina
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.k3vcyn6

steps.instruction: Prepared with deionised water. A) 0.2 M of NaH 2 PO 4 (Sodium phosphate monobasic anhydrous, CAS Number: 7558-80-7 ) BioUltra, ≥99.0% (T) (Sigma-Aldrich S3139). B) 0.2 M of Na 2 HPO 4 (Sodium phosphate dibasic anhydrous, CAS Number: 7558-79-4) Ultra ≥99.5%, (Sigma-Aldrich S7907). To make 200 mL of phosphate buffer solution (0.1 M, pH 7.2), mix 28 mL of stock solution A + 72 mL of stock solution B and 100 mL of deionised water. Adjust pH with solutions of 1N HCl or 5 M NaOH if necessary.

substeps.instruction: M of NaH 2 PO 4 (Sodium phosphate monobasic anhydrous, CAS Number: 7558-80-7 ) BioUltra, ≥99.0% (T) (Sigma-Aldrich S3139). B)
M of Na 2 HPO 4 (Sodium phosphate dibasic anhydrous, CAS Number: 7558-79-4) Ultra ≥99.5%, (Sigma-Aldrich S7907). To make 200 mL of phosphate buffer solution (
M, pH 7.2), mix 28 mL of stock solution A + 72 mL of stock solution B and 100 mL of deionised water. Adjust pH with solutions of 1N HCl or 5 M NaOH if necessary.

steps.instruction: To prepare 100 mL Mix 10 mL of glutaraldehyde solution (25% for electron Microscopy, Merck, Germany) with 90 ml of phosphate buffer Sorensen's solution (0.1 M, pH 7.2). 1% Osmium tetroxide in deionised water Dissolve 0.25 g of OsO 4 in 25 mL of deionised water (use fume hood). Protect the solution from light and store at 4 °C.

Critical/point dried and SEM observations. Samples were critically-point dried using a Samdri-780 (Tousim Research Corporation, Rockville, USA), mounted on cooper studs with a carbon conductive tape, double coated (Ted Pella I.N.C U.S.A) and then sputter-coated with gold-palladium with a Fine coat ion sputter (JFC-1100, Jeol Ltd., Tokyo, Japan). Observations were performed using a JEOL JSM 6390 scanning electron microscope operated at 25 kV.
